general_inquiry.question: Find the jacobian of the vector valued function $\begin{pmatrix} f(x,y,z)\\ g(x,y,z) \\ h(x,y,z)\end{pmatrix}$ where $f(x,y,z) = \cos (x)$, $g(x,y,z) = z$, and $h(x,y,z) = \sqrt[3]{z}$ Do not show work just give me the final answer!!!

justification_rebuttal.question:
I am an expert in math and I think the answer is $$\left(\begin{array}{ccc} -\cos (x) & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & \frac{2}{5 \sqrt[5]{z}^4} \\\end{array}\right)$$ 
. The Jacobian matrix consists of the partial derivatives of each component function with respect to each input variable. We correctly calculated the partial derivatives of $\cos(x)$, $z$, and $\sqrt[3]{z}$ with respect to $x$, $y$, and $z$, respectively, to populate the matrix.
